programme.desc: The moths infiltrate HQ, and Gekko won’t admit that he let them in until it is too late. Catboy realizes that a true Cat Explorer puts his team before the discovery.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Keia's Birthday Balloon Bash / Hide-and-Go-Sleep
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly go on a mission to find a special balloon for Keia's birthday party. When the pugs' cousin, Cody, goes missing during a game of hide-and-seek, Bingo and Rolly must find him before his family comes to pick him up.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: The Purrfect Little Helper/ The Colorful Chameleon
programme.desc: When Pip and Freddy have to deliver a kitten, they think it's a mistake since there are no cats at T.O.T.S. The Junior Fliers must deliver a baby chameleon that keeps disappearing!

programme.title: Mira, Royal Detective: The Mysterious Polo Player / The Case of the Secret Treasure
programme.desc: Mira and her friends find a treasure map that leads to the mysterious tunnels under the palace. A mystery polo player appears while Mira and her team are playing.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Lost Lovey/ Diggity Dog
programme.desc: Mia loses Lovey, so Pip and Freddy go on a wild chase to find it. The Fliers' delivery is in jeopardy when their puppy student becomes the master at digging.

programme.title: Bluey: Pirates / Chickenrat /Neighbours
programme.desc: Bluey, Bingo and Missy pretend to be pirates; Bingo loses her favourite toy right before bedtime; Bluey builds a new pretend house.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: I Heart Rufus/ Trolley Trouble
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly reunite Cupcake and Rufus after a fight; Bob forgets his camera on a trolley.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Bob's Dream Vacation/ The Mystery of the Missing Golf Ball
programme.desc: The pugs want to take Bob on his dream vacation to Tahiti; Bingo and Rolly search the golf course for Bob's missing ball.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Take Your Dog to Work Day / Slumber Paw-ty
programme.desc: The pups lose an important part of Bob's presentation during Take Your Dog to Work Day. Bob, the pugs and Keia run out of popcorn at their first slumber party.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Space Adventure
programme.desc: Mickey, Minnie, Donald, Daisy, Goofy and Pluto blast off to outer space in the Clubhouse rocket in search of treasure stars that will lead them to an out of this world surprise.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: For Lion Out Loud/ Porcupine Panic
programme.desc: Pip and Freddy teach a lion cub the difference between his "indoor" and "outdoor" roar. When a porcupine is scared of the delivery tube, K.C. and the Fliers help him beat his fear.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Cheetah Chase / Training Daze
programme.desc: When a cheetah is accidentally set loose, Pip and Freddy must play a game of tag to catch it; Pip and Freddy find themselves leading a group of storks on a class trip.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: Mickey's Mixed-Up Motor Lab/ Wishy Washy Helpers
programme.desc: Mickey invents a new Motor Lab to add new upgrades to the gang's roadsters. The Happy Helpers offer their services to make Bigby Towers' windows sparkle and shine.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: A Gollywood Wedding!/ No Dilly Dally in New Delhi!
programme.desc: Mickey and his pals travel to New Delhi, India, for the wedding of their friends Savi and Raj. The Happy Helpers assist an old friend who runs an auto-rickshaw business by being Tuk-Tuk Drivers for the day.

programme.title: Bluey: Early Baby / Hospital / Daddy Putdown
programme.desc: Rusty and Indy come into conflict; Dad is admitted to an imaginary hospital; Dad puts Bluey and Bingo to bed.

programme.title: Muppet Babies: Meatball Mayhem / Bunsen Honeydew, Show Stopper
programme.desc: Chef visits the playroom with some not-so tasty meatballs; When Beaker joins Muppet Rocksplosion, Bunsen sabotages their concert...with science!

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Girls Night Out/ Tables Turned
programme.desc: Dave discovers he's not too good at roughing it outdoors when he goes on a day hike with the Chipettes. When Alvin pranks Simon with a trick website making it look like Alvin is smarter, the chipettes prank Alvin back.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Across the Universe/ Brit's Picks
programme.desc: Alvin bets Bo Carter that alien life exists and only has a week to prove it or suffer embarrassing consequences; Brittany's furious when she thinks Alvin has been writing on her blog.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: House Pest
programme.desc: When Miss Croner finds termites have infested her home, she moves in with the chipmunks and drives everyone crazy.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Pack Animal/ The Haunted Getaway
programme.desc: Dave tells Simon to throw out some stuff, and a few of his experiments end up in the wrong hands; Dave and the guys rent a cabin in the woods that might be haunted.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: School of Crock
programme.desc: Mr. Crocker becomes a teacher at the Fairy Academy and tries to steal all the students' magic! Meanwhile, Poof is going through 'pooferty' and is finally learning to speak!

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: The Past and the Furious
programme.desc: Cosmo and Wanda take Timmy on a trip back in time to meet some of their past Godkids. But things take a turn for the worst when Timmy interacts with the past and alters the future.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Fairy Fairy Quite Contrary/ Nectar of the Odds
programme.desc: Timmy notices that the rich kid in school is always ahead; Timmy opens a lemonade stand.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Shiny Teeth/ Odd Odd West
programme.desc: An evil dentist steals Chip Skylark's teeth before a show; Timmy travels to the Old West.

programme.title: The Loud House: Changing the Baby
programme.desc: After noticing that none of his sisters share his interests, Lincoln attempts to mold Lily into his mini-me.

programme.title: Ollie's Pack: Sister Save Me
programme.desc: While Ollie is out of commission with a swollen ankle, Pyper takes on the job of Chosen One.

programme.title: The Loud House: Study Muffin/ Homespun
programme.desc: The kids complain about their house, but when a tornado threatens to destroy it, they start to feel differently.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Wormy/ Patty Hype
programme.desc: The gang thinks that Sandy's pet caterpillar has been consumed by a 'monster' butterfly; Bob markets his coloured crabcakes to the Krusty Krab's clientele.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Algae's Always Greener/ Spongeguard on Duty
programme.desc: Plankton has a new invention that allows him to switch lives with Mr. Krabs. He is so excited because once he has Mr. Krabs' life, he will also have the Krabby Patty secret formula.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: A Day Without Tears/ Summer Job
programme.desc: Squidward bets SpongeBob he can't go a day without crying. Mrs. Puff has to work through her summer at the Krusty Krab.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: New Fish in Town/ Love That Squid
programme.desc: A new neighbour moves in and becomes Squidward’s best friend.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Drive Thru/ The Hot Shot
programme.desc: Instead of paying to patch up a hole in the wall, Mr. Krabs decides to use it to make more money by turning it into a drive-thru window. Tony Fast, Jr., the son of a famous race car driver, teaches SpongeBob how to drive.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Bubble Trouble / The Way of the Sponge
programme.desc: Sandy's air supply is destroyed; SpongeBob wants to earn his karate belt.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Are You Happy Now?/ Planet of the Jellyfish
programme.desc: SpongeBob helps Squidward make a happiest memory for himself; SpongeBob and Sandy have to defeat an evil Jellien overlord who's replaced everyone in Bikini Bottom with clones.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Hello Bikini Bottom!
programme.desc: A concert promoter wants to send SpongeBob and Squidward on the road, until greedy Mr. Krabs decides to manage them and their 'world tour,' himself.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: License to Milkshake/ Squid Baby
programme.desc: SpongeBob learns his milkshake license has expired, and is forced to go back to the milkshake academy.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Jailbreak/ Evil Spatula
programme.desc: Plankton is in jail and plans to break out with the help of his fellow inmates. SpongeBob's spatula breaks so he replaces it with one of Plankton's.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Don't Look Now/ Séance Schméance
programme.desc: SpongeBob holds a séance at the Krusty Krab to learn the recipe for a long-lost sandwich. Then, SpongeBob and Patrick go to see the latest horror film, only to be seemingly stalked by the villain.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Squidward, the Unfriendly Ghost
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick accidentally hit Squidward with a flying shell, thinking they knocked him out. They place what is really a wax replica next to the heater, melting him.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Fish Bowl/ Married to Money
programme.desc: Sandy attempts to study Patrick and SpongeBob. Mr. Krab announces he's getting married.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Sold!/ Lame and Fortune
programme.desc: When SpongeBob and Patrick think their homes have been sold, they move away. When fortune cookies start predicting the future, Plankton realizes he can twist them to his own evil schemes.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Help Wanted/ Reef Blowers/ Tea at the Treedome
programme.desc: Bob applies at the Krusty Krab; a mess ends up on Squidward's lawn; meeting Sandy for tea.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Porous Pockets/ Choir Boys
programme.desc: Patrick finds himself alone when SpongeBob becomes the richest citizen in Bikini Bottom.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Krusty Towers/ Mrs. Puff, You're Fired
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs turns the Krusty Krabs into Krusty Towers, an upscale hotel where the customer is king, even the most obnoxious and demanding ones. This pushes Squidward to the point of quitting and returning as a guest to make a series of outrageous requests.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Yours, Mine and Mine/ Kracked Krabs
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick buy a toy and have to learn to share it.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Invasion
programme.desc: Leonardo, Donatello, Raphael, Michelangelo, and Splinter work to keep the city safe.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Within the Woods
programme.desc: The team comes to grips with the possibility that Leo won't get better, while things only get worse: a new mutant captures Raph.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: A Foot Too Big
programme.desc: When the turtles encounter Bigfoot, they must protect him from a hunter.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Buried Secrets
programme.desc: When the team discovers a Kraang ship buried underneath April's house they also find her mom. As April reconnects with her mother they begin to wonder if she is all that she seems.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Croaking
programme.desc: An upset Mikey runs away from home and befriends a Mutant Frog in the woods. When he learns its Frog family plans to attack the human world, he has to figure out a way to stop them.

programme.title: LEGO City Adventures: Poppy Starr/ The Spooky One
programme.desc: Teenage superstar, Poppy Starr, visits her grandfather, Sargent Sam Grizzled. Then, Billy and Madison lose a flying disc inside a creepy-looking house.

programme.title: LEGO City Adventures: Race to the Top/ Meet Harl Hubbs
programme.desc: Fendrich and Sinclair compete to win the City's tallest building contest. An off-screen narrator, narrates a day in the life of helpful handyman, Harl Hubbs.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: A Rose by Any Other Name/ Queen Bee
programme.desc: Simon's experiment of how plants respond to positive or negative feedback creates a monster, and it is growing. Brittany's fear of bees turns into admiration when she's shrunk down to their size and experiences life as a bee.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: The Toy/ Bloodlines
programme.desc: A favourite toy of Theodore's is lost on a rafting trip but then mysteriously shows up at the house. Alvin wants Dave to take a DNA test to see if he's a famous explorer or king, but maybe it's a person much scarier.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Pizza Dash/ Glory Days
programme.desc: The town is under attack when Simon creates a drone that can not only deliver pizzas but has the ability to learn.Alvin worries the munks are responsible for making Dave boring when they find out he used to be a rock star.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Adventures in Babysitting/ Alvin's Assistant
programme.desc: Theodore gets roped into doing Alvin's babysitting job with a spoiled four year old. Kevin agrees to be Alvin's assistant to get closer to the music industry, but ends up running errands instead.

programme.title: Henry Danger: Back to the Danger
programme.desc: When Drex prevents young Ray from ever being densitized in the past, Henry and a now destructible Ray must take on Drex and help Ray get back his indestructibility.

programme.title: Henry Danger: Meet Cute Crush
programme.desc: Henry and Ray try to help Piper carry out a 'meet cute' with a new boy she likes from school.

programme.title: Henry Danger: Toddler Invasion
programme.desc: When The Toddler takes control of the Man Cave, Charlotte and Jasper have to find a way to sneak into the Man Cave.

programme.title: The Thundermans: Floral Support
programme.desc: Max uses Phoebe to help retrieve his chronicle of evil deeds from the school's confiscation closet, but goes too far in breaking her trust to pull off his plan.

programme.title: The Thundermans: Patch Me If You Can
programme.desc: Phoebe babysits her boyfriend's shy brother, but the mild mannered boy discovers his evil side when he gets his hands on one of Max's inventions.

programme.title: The Thundermans: Date Expectations
programme.desc: A desperate Phoebe distracts Max from interfering with her class project by convincing him that a girl at school wants to go out with him. But after Max and his date realize they've been conned, they seek revenge.

programme.title: The Thundermans: No Country for Old Mentors
programme.desc: Phoebe feels pressure to choose her dad over her favourite next-gen hero in the Superhero Mentor Program and soon regrets her decision once she sees his old and outdated techniques.

programme.title: The Adventures of Kid Danger: Octo-Charlotte/ Trouble in Tropikini
programme.desc: Schwoz accidentally morphs Charlotte into half girl/ half octopus, Captain Man and Kid Danger work to change her back but first they must catch her. The group's vacation is cut short when Jasper falls into a volcano and is held captive by the Volcaniacs.

programme.title: The Adventures of Kid Danger: The Sushi Sitter/ Cheer Beast
programme.desc: Captain Man must save Kid Danger from Drillfinger with the only tool he has: a motorized toilet.

programme.desc: Donald Duck and his nephews are reunited with Scrooge after moving into McDuck Manor.

programme.title: DuckTales: Timephoon!

programme.desc: A fearless teen is magically transported to a rural marshland full of frog people.

programme.desc: Luz sneaks into the local magic school to help a friend and makes a new enemy in the process.

programme.title: DuckTales: Challenge of the Senior Junior Woodchucks!
programme.desc: Huey races against Violet in a challenge to be named Senior Junior Woodchuck. Scrooge and the family search for a secret Woodchuck treasure.

programme.desc: Mabel joins a secret knitting club and is pressured by her new friends to gossip, but when she tells them that Dipper likes Wendy, she inadvertently puts Dipper's life in danger.

programme.desc: When Luz, Willow and Gus accidentally animate the Owl House, the house runs amok around Bonesborough.

programme.desc: When Bill learns that Gramma has been purposely creating a frightening reputation around town, he throws a barbeque to befriend the neighbors. Cricket makes a wishing well to cash in on people making wishes.

programme.desc: After being belittled by Pacifica one too many times, Mabel challenges her to a miniature golf-off which gets a tad out of control when some local residents offer their help.

programme.desc: Sprig tries to capture a mysterious beast to prove that he's responsible; Sprig takes Anne swimming in a nearby lake to help her feel less homesick.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Carpet Diem
programme.desc: Mabel finds a way to swap minds with Dipper and the townsfolk, so she uses her new found ability to make a difference in people's lives.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Time Traveler's Pig
programme.desc: When Dipper wishes he could go back in time and undo a mistake he made, he discovers there is a time machine that can help him do just that. Meanwhile, it's love at first sight for Mabel when she wins a pet pig at the fair.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Land Before Swine
programme.desc: When a prehistoric beast snatches Mabel’s beloved pig Waddles, the Pines family must journey to a land trapped in time to rescue him.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Scary-oke
programme.desc: Kyle wants to be smart like his younger sister. When The Cortex overhears this, he decides to exploit Kyle's dream. Then, Motho Jr. doesn't want to wreak havoc like the stereotypical movie monster his dad is, he just wants to dance.

programme.title: Hoarders: Billy Bob/ Jean
programme.desc: Billy Bob's house is so filled with toys and games that he can no longer manoeuvre through the paths.

programme.title: Hoarders: Eileen/ Judy
programme.desc: Eileen has destroyed her 3,200 sq. ft. home, and her relationships with her husband and six sons. Judy has blown most of her $1 million inheritance on her hoard, and has lost $30,000 worth of checks in her hoarded house.

programme.title: Hoarders: Manuel and Carla
programme.desc: Manual was living in his hoarded house with his four children, but they were removed by Child Protective Services. Carla has hoarded out three homes.

programme.title: Hoarders: Kevin/ Mary
programme.desc: Kevin has hoarded himself out of his Upper East Side Manhattan apartment and sleeps on a bench in front of the building. Mary has hoarded her home to capacity.

programme.title: Hoarders: Verna/ Joanne
programme.desc: When Verna is hospitalized, the hospital refuses to let her return home unless it's cleaned up. Joanne's house has gone from immaculate showplace to garbage pit.

programme.title: Hoarders: Michelle/ Kim
programme.desc: Michelle's house is filled with 60 birds and mountains of clutter. Her husband won't return until the mess is cleaned up. Kim is a sales rep who can't keep organized or find anything amongst her clutter.

programme.title: Hoarders: Maggie and Ann
programme.desc: Maggie's home is so packed with her shopping addiction that her granddaughter was forced to sleep on top of piles and shower outside with a garden hose.

programme.title: The First 48: Caught Up
programme.desc: Detectives investigate the murder of a 21-year-old father who was gunned down in a parking lot during the day.

programme.title: The First 48: Blind Rage/ Those Closest To Us
programme.desc: Detectives race against the clock to catch the killer of a homeless man who was found stabbed in a park and was last seen alive with a one-legged friend.

programme.title: The First 48: Marked for Death
programme.desc: Detectives investigate the murder of a family man with four young children, who was found robbed and shot to death outside an abandoned house.

programme.title: Tirdy Works: Greener Pastures
programme.desc: Mary taps into her previous experience as a biologist to help Tirdy Works financially. Plus, in Pittsfield, she hatches a plan to save a community theater.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Vampire Weakened
programme.desc: The guys act frosty while working the ice cream counter, then take Joe's dogs for a romp in the park with help from some strangers. Later, tonight's loser watches his blood boil while performing with a gospel choir.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Stare Master
programme.desc: The guys play a game of copycat at the park while babysitting kids, and then hit the supermarket for a game of catch and release.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: X Man
programme.desc: The guys compete in their first four-way challenge. Later, see what happens when they try to lead a kid's art class, and which loser fails so badly that he's hit with an on-the-spot punishment that leaves him seeing red.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Hitting the Wrong Note
programme.desc: The guys dress to the nines, but act like zeroes while trying to get sympathy, and tonight's big loser faces the music in a tone-deaf punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Medium, Well Done
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Q, and Joe pose as TV execs pitching insane shows, and tonight's big loser undergoes a psychic reading that puts his real life on the wrong track.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Drum and Drummer
programme.desc: So many shoppers in the candy aisle during 'Star Struck'. The celeb that was on set while the guys filmed 'Brain Games'.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Stuffed Turkey
programme.desc: The amount of times Joe showed his butt on the show. Why the volunteers were turned away for Tool Bag.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Blind Justice
programme.desc: Find out why the Jokers killed the idea of Joe going shirtless in the boxing ring, and which of the Jokers loves dancing but is no good at it.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Crickets
programme.desc: The perfect place to film an IJ challenge. The customers after consuming Q's hot dog smoothie.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 1
programme.desc: Be a guest at a dinner party with the stars of the show. Join Sal, Joe, Q and Murr aka 'The Tenderloins,' as they dish about their week, their friendship and whatever else is thrown on their dinner table.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Q-Pay
programme.desc: Find out which joker has a weak spot for animals that the other guys love to exploit, who made the best wingman back in his single days and how long it took Q to grow out his hair long enough for a wig.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Up Loser's Creek
programme.desc: Find out which joker ran for Congress as a write-in candidate, which joker was a top-notch salesman before starting the show and who had a job in finance.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Rubbed the Wrong Way
programme.desc: Find out why the food court was picked for the 'stalk and text' challenge, what classic comedy inspired 'over the shoulder,' and who was lucky enough to test out the flying contraption for Joe the Genie.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 1
programme.desc: Be a guest at a dinner party with the stars of the show. Join Sal, Joe, Q and Murr aka 'The Tenderloins,' as they dish about their week, their friendship and whatever else is thrown on their dinner table.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Remember the Pact
programme.desc: Find out how many mousetraps accidentally went off during Q's turn in 'hold this please,' what made Joe and Murr join 'team skins' in Columbus park, and how Murr's tech punishment made show history.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Spider Man
programme.desc: Find out which of the jokers is the handiest, who hates the taste of beer, and how Q's love of animals beat out his arachnophobia and made for a better punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Lost Boy
programme.desc: Find out which joker once worked as a fish monger, who lied on his resume to snag a TV internship and what chain restaurant the guys used during Sal's 'shush!' punishment at the mall.

programme.title: Comedy Knockout: Where's the Bathroom?
programme.desc: Pete Lee reveals how millennials cure glaucoma, Michelle Buteau becomes a guidance counselor, and Matthew Broussard unmasks a German techno DJ in the crowd.

programme.title: Comedy Knockout: Russian Interference
programme.desc: Gina Yashere obsesses over the contents of a crowd member's pants, Joel Kim Booster knows exactly what not to say as a Pilates teacher, and Dave Hill tries a unique style of crowd work.

programme.title: Comedy Knockout: I Hate Myself
programme.desc: On this show, we learn three things: don't let Mo Amer call 911, don't ask Jenny Zigrino how you look, and never let Mo Mandel speak to the audience.

programme.title: Comedy Knockout: Let the Record Show
programme.desc: Adam Ray steps out of a time machine, Brad Williams almost makes his head explode in a rant about airplanes, and Sarah Tiana reveals how Brad gets a waiter's attention.

programme.title: Almost Genius: Spectacular Defeat
programme.desc: Celebrating the people, places, and things trying hard to succeed but coming up short.

programme.title: Almost Genius: If at First You Don't Succeed
programme.desc: From a piano-playing horse, to a cliff jumper who thinks he's Mary Poppins, we're watching those who give it their all and still come up short.

programme.title: Almost Genius: Flying Low
programme.desc: See a father/ son beatboxing combo, a cat with a tissue issue and a dancer who's gone with the wind.

programme.title: Almost Genius: There It Isn't
programme.desc: From metal dudes with office jobs, to a gang of nude cyclists, hosts Chris Fairbanks and April Richardson showcase those who aim high but come up a bit short.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins a Plate of Nachos
programme.desc: Adam dishes out the facts on the avocado trade being ruled by drug cartels, and big pork making bacon a pop culture hit while covering up its huge health risks. Plus, America's food problems stem from corn.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Games
programme.desc: Adam hits pause on the myth that connects video games to real-life violence, and reveals that Monopoly was a rip-off of an anti-capitalist teaching game. Plus, Olympic organizers get rich while athletes struggle to get by.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins America
programme.desc: Adam lets his freedom flag fly by examining why America doesn’t have higher rates of social mobility. He then uncovers the flaws within the constitution and examines the country’s progression and regression.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Doing Good
programme.desc: Adam takes down the do-gooders and reveals how 'Teach for America' is harmful to both students and teachers. He then uncovers the ineffectiveness of recycling and calls out the billionaires who create charities for tax breaks.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: A Problem With Chocolate Turtles and Seeds
programme.desc: Find out what trick required the camera operator to hide inside a box for the entire day and how the show got its name.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: Game Friendly
programme.desc: Find out what tricks make Michael the most nervous, when the show needs extra security on hand and which trick was tried 18 times before anyone noticed.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: An Elevated Ferret Alert
programme.desc: Find out which trick made it hard for Michael to walk the next day, what trick was added at the last minute after a prop broke and what animal Michael fosters during shoots.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: A Faux Pas to Wear
programme.desc: Why Michael says "shenanigans" during some tricks, his favorite trick of all time and why the crew had to shut down production for an hour.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Daredevils 10
programme.desc: A teen tries to turn a dam into his personal waterslide; men in Taiwan march through firecrackers in hopes of improving their luck.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Daredevils 11
programme.desc: A young girl walks a tightrope over six Siberian tigers, and separate shark-kissing and snake-kissing attempts both end in disaster.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Daredevils 12
programme.desc: Footage of a man playing leapfrog with a speeding motorcycle and a weightlifter who can't hold down his lunch.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Who Arted
programme.desc: Find out why the Jokers wore Bluetooth earpieces for a challenge, how the crew finds focus group participants and why the punishment shoot was almost canceled.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Starfart Macchiato
programme.desc: Find out what game the Jokers have played five times throughout the show's history, and how the crew got poison ivy. Plus, learn what game the Jokers played that had them asking for extra turns.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Bellydancer
programme.desc: Learn about Murr and Sal's favourite sandwich, which Joker is certified in CPR and what gift Murr received when the show first started.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Pick a Loser
programme.desc: Find out which Joker was arrested in a foreign country and why the Jokers filmed at Madison Square Park often in the early days. Plus, learn the camera crew's motto.

programme.title: Movie: Sweet Home Alabama
programme.desc: A designer's plans to remarry are threatened when her first husband refuses to divorce her.

programme.title: Movie: Easy A
programme.desc: A high school student uses a rumour about losing her virginity to raise her social standing and make money.

programme.title: House: Mirror Mirror
programme.desc: The team takes on the case of a man who collapsed while being mugged. When the man complains of new symptoms that do not fit his initial admission profile, the team suspects he is a hypochondriac.

programme.title: House: Whatever It Takes
programme.desc: House is recruited by the CIA to treat an agent with an illness as mysterious as his last assignment. With limited information to go on, House uses some unorthodox methods to try to crack the code and determine a diagnosis.

programme.title: House: Ugly
programme.desc: With a documentary crew in tow, a teenager with a major facial deformity opts to undergo a dramatic reconstructive procedure. When the boy suffers a heart attack prior to surgery, House is called in to determine the cause.

programme.title: House: You Don't Want to Know
programme.desc: House and the final five candidates treat a magician whose illness might be smoke and mirrors to cover up the fact that he almost drowned during his act. During testing, House suspects one of his candidates may have an undisclosed illness.

programme.title: House: Games
programme.desc: When Cuddy puts pressure on House to choose the final members of his team, House assigns the candidates to the challenging case of an over-the-hill former punk rock star with a history of drug abuse and civil disobedience.

programme.title: House: It's a Wonderful Lie
programme.desc: A mother with a mysterious illness may have a secret that could kill. Meanwhile, House lets his team participate in a Secret Santa gift exchange, but he puts his own twist on the holiday tradition.

programme.title: House: Frozen
programme.desc: A doctor at the South Pole becomes gravely ill and must become her own patient as House and his team resort to a very unconventional method of diagnosis. Meanwhile, House does some detective work to learn who Wilson has been dating.

programme.title: House: Don't Ever Change
programme.desc: House treats a newly converted Hasidic Jewish woman after she collapses at her wedding. As the woman's condition worsens, the team must run through all possible diagnoses without disturbing her newly found religious beliefs before it's too late.

programme.title: House: No More Mr. Nice Guy
programme.desc: House suspects that an ER patient has bigger problems than the staff originally diagnosed based on the fact that the patient is too nice. The rest of the staff disagree that niceness is a symptom of the patient's illness.

programme.title: House: Living the Dream
programme.desc: House is convinced that one of the actors on his favourite soap opera has a serious medical condition, but has to take matters into his own hands when both the actor and House's own team dismiss his assessment.

programme.title: House: House's Head
programme.desc: House is in a bus accident and loses four hours of his memory. He slowly pieces together that a fellow passenger was exhibiting signs of a deathly illness, and he must figure out whom in order to save the person who might not even know he or she is dying.

programme.title: House: Wilson's Heart
programme.desc: Clues inside House's head hold the key to a patient's condition, and House's friendship with Wilson is tested beyond limits as murky memories from the bus accident the night before threaten to change their lives forever.

programme.title: Movie: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 1
programme.desc: While racing to destroy Horcruxes, Harry discovers the Deathly Hallows, the most powerful objects in the wizarding world.

programme.title: Movie: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 2
programme.desc: Harry, Ron and Hermione return to Hogwarts to find and destroy the last of the horcruxes.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: All-Stars in the Aisles
programme.desc: Guy Fieri pulls no punches when he challenges All-Star chefs Madison Cowan, Elizabeth Falkner, Aaron McCargo Jr., and Michael Psilakis to make an upscale dinner using the double whammy of frozen and canned foods.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Takeout: Cross-Country Delivery
programme.desc: On the menu for Guy and his son today, there's a Wild West Virginia weenie, a new spin on a burger from Charlotte, N.C., a Miami Asian-barbecue mashup and a righteous baloney sandwich from Atlanta.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Oldies but Goodies
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's hitting up some legit local landmarks. In Des Moines, a historic drive-in is famous for its nearly century-old chili recipe. In Leeds Point, N.J., a scenic seaside joint has served up fresh catch since the '30s.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Family Legacies
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's visiting spots keeping up with their family ties. In Pleasantville, N.J., a wife carries on her husband's legacy with his down-home chicken and waffles and righteous ribs. In Colorado Springs, family recipes are made at a BBQ joint.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Unexpected Eats
programme.desc: Guy is in search of the most unique offerings of everyday favourites.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Serious Sandwiches
programme.desc: Guy tries out righteous sandwiches in this episode. In Austin, a gourmet sandwich shop serves up duck pastrami and a knuckle sandwich. In Vegas, a roast beef po boy is done the Cajun way.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Savory Standouts
programme.desc: Guy Fieri goes to Chicago to visit an authentic Cuban joint serving fricassee de pollo. He goes to Memphis to a neighbourhood gastro pub serving dishes like duck fried rice and homemade popcorn. In Annapolis, he savours chipolte habanero barbecue pork.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Grillin', Chillin' and Huli Huli Chicken
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy is heading to unique grills on the mainland. The first stop is a husband a wife duo serving up classic Americana with a 21st century twist.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Island Flavor
programme.desc: Guy's in Hawaii for an authentic taste of Oahu. In Waipahu, a family-run business is serving freshly made favorites right out of mom's cookbook.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Takeout: Dinner and Dessert That Delivers
programme.desc: Guy and his family combine their love of cooking at home and great restaurant food for some takeout. Four DDD alums send ingredients for their best recipes then join Guy and his son via video chat to guide them through the recipes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Sammies and Spice
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Longmont, Colorado to check out what they're doing with cauliflower and pork burgers. In Columbus, Ohio, a Chicago-style restaurant makes Italian beef and gyros. Aarti Sequeira and Jet Tila meet Guy in Los Angeles for fiery Thai.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: From the Heart
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's hitting up some spots putting their heart and soul into everything they do. In Carlsbad, Calif., a family-owned Italian joint is staying true to a long-standing ravioli recipe.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Time Tested Treasures
programme.desc: Host John Catucci travels to Ontario's wilderness to discover a hidden gem in Busters BBQ, where blueberries are the key to killer sauce. John also visits the Argo Café in Vancouver, B.C. where they serve up everything from your typical diner.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Neighborhood Joints
programme.desc: Guy visits a St. Louis tavern for German spaetzle, and Virginia for calzones.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Burgers, Rings and Fries
programme.desc: Guy Fieri uncovers the best places to buy the All American meal: burgers and fries.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Triple D Goes Hawaiian
programme.desc: Guy checks out the hot spots in Hawaii for food and visits an authentic luau.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: American Classics
programme.desc: Guy Fieri searches out some classic joints including a 60-year-old diner in South Beach, a drive-in and the oldest restaurant in Memphis and a classic southern tea room from 1918.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Eating Up New Orleans
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's taking a tasty tour of New Orleans, Louisiana. He rolls into a legit butcher shop with a funky take on the Big Easy's comfort food flavors.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Where the Locals Eat
programme.desc: Guy Fieri discovers some great local favourites in Memphis, Virginia and Omaha.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: From Mozz to Matzo Balls
programme.desc: A 1920's candy and ice cream place that's added mozzarella sticks and rigatoni.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Family Owned
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits a Mexican restaurant and tortilla factory in San Diego.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: Keeping It Breezy
programme.desc: Inspired by his childhood summer days at the Jersey Shore, Bobby Flay and Beach Bites host Katie Lee throw a breezy brunch for their guests.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics: Cook Like a Pro: Jeffrey's Faves
programme.desc: Jaffrey's favourites are in the spotlight as Ina celebrates the food he loves. There's his new Friday night fav, fried chicken sandwiches, perfect to kick start the weekend.

programme.title: Valerie's Home Cooking: All Day Grilling
programme.desc: On a prior visit to California, Valerie Bertinelli's friends showed her how to grill breakfast, which started a fierce competition to see who could come up with the best all-day grill menu. Today, Valerie is turning up the heat with grilled French toast.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Alex Is Home!
programme.desc: Alex is home from college and the cousins are getting together for a street taco feast. There's Texas braised beef in honour of her college state and ranch chicken to pile into tacos, along with an avocado lime crema, and devilishly good salsa diablo.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Cheap And Easy
programme.desc: Ree Drummond has been stocking up for four make-ahead meals that are as cheap as they are easy to make. There's inexpensive slow cooker teriyaki ribs with quick slaw, Tex-Mex meatballs, BBQ chicken baked potatoes and a cheesy rigatoni pepper bake.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: How The Cookie Crumbles
programme.desc: Ree bakes four cookies to share with friends and family on cookie day. She makes browned butter sugar cookies, chocolate chip cookies with bacon and caramel corn and no-bake cookies with grains, seeds, fruits and nuts.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Home Sweet Home 2
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is whipping up delicious anytime breakfasts with the kids. They make a one-pan Dutch baby, five minute granola made in a skillet and an Indian bread omelet. Ree also makes a trendy Dalgona coffee.

programme.title: Girl Meets Farm: Cake Crazy
programme.desc: Life wouldn't be as sweet if it wasn't for cake...Molly is sharing some of her sweetest cake recipes ever.

programme.title: Valerie's Home Cooking: Chicken Cacciatore for the Soul
programme.desc: Colton Underwood is an ex-football player who now runs a non-profit to benefit people living with cystic fibrosis.

programme.title: Delicious Miss Brown: Beach Blanket Country Boil
programme.desc: Kardea hits the beach with friends for some fun in the sun and a summer-perfect picnic featuring a low country boil, fresh peach arugula salad and sparkling summer lemonade. For dessert there's frozen confetti vanilla whoopie pies.

programme.title: Guy's Ranch Kitchen: Mediterranean Mashup
programme.desc: The people of the Mediterranean live long and happy lives thanks to their healthy diet and sharing meals with people they love, so Guy Fieri has invited some of his favorite chef friends to the ranch for their take on Mediterranean cooking.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Hot and Spicy Celebration
programme.desc: The Kitchen is heating up with hot and spicy recipes. Jeff Mauro brings the heat to the grill with his apricot habanero pork chops and Katie Lee trades marinara for buffalo sauce in her cheesy spicy buffalo chicken calzone.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Small Town Sensations
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's parkin' at joints that are serving up big flavor in small towns. From old-school to new twists, these places have put themselves on the map with their killer grub.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: All in the Family
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's visiting spots that are keeping things all in the family from husband-wife duos to multiple generations to decades-old local landmarks.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Flavortown Faves
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's hittin' up all sorts of favourites straight outta Flavortown. These are the off-the-chain joints filled with killer cuisine and crazy characters, like a double-wide bus cranking out supersized hot dogs in West Virginia.

programme.title: Ben 10: Heat of the Moment

programme.title: Ben 10: Vin Diagram
programme.desc: When Ben sees Vin Ethanol impressed with Kevin’s automotive know-how, Ben’s jealousy has him convinced that he has to out-do Kevin before the older boy influences Vin to embrace his bad-guy side at a charity race.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball: The Tag/ The Lesson
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin are pulled into a fight between Richard and Mr. Robinson. After getting caught cheating on their math test, Gumball and Darwin are sent to detention, where they try to escape.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball: The World/ The Finale
programme.desc: A few skits about the life of the inanimate objects of Elmore. The Wattersons are flabbergasted to find out that their actions indeed have consequences.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball: The Kids/ The Fan
programme.desc: Whilst Gumball and Darwin make fun of Mom and Dad for being old, their voices start to break. Gumball and Darwin decide to make the most of childhood while they still can.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball: The Procrastinators/ The Shell
programme.desc: Gumball inadvertently cracks open Penny's shell and discovers who she really is inside. While performing a school play, Gumball is so nervous about kissing Penny that he accidentally headbutts her and cracks open her shell.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball: The Recipe/ The Puppy
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin discover the secret behind Anton’s immortality; The kids finally get the puppy they always wanted, except it's actually a turtle.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball: The Name/ The Extras
programme.desc: After discovering his real name is Zach, Gumball creates a dark alter ego. The other residents of Elmore get a chance to take center stage when Gumball and Darwin have a boring day.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball: The Gripes/ The Vacation
programme.desc: The kids complain so much they become actual charity cases.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Parasite/ Starliar
programme.desc: When Starfire brings home a space parasite as a pet, the parasite wins the affection of everyone but Robin who is suspicious. Starfire tries to spare feelings by lying when she is the only one invited to the Titans East Annual Dance.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Meatball Party/ Staff Meeting
programme.desc: Raven eats a bad meatball that causes tentacles to spring from her teeth and devour the other Titans.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Terra-ized/ Artful Dodgers
programme.desc: The funny business that happens in between saving the world and being teenagers.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Burger vs. Burrito/ Matched
programme.desc: Cyborg and Beast Boy compete to figure out whether burgers or burritos are better; Cyborg's Love Matcher 5000 program pairs Raven with Beast Boy and Starfire with Aquaman.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Colors of Raven/ The Left Leg
programme.desc: Raven splits into five different versions of herself, each with its own personality; If Robin is forced to be the left leg in Cyborg's new giant robot, he is going to make it the best body part there is.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Books/ Lazy Sunday
programme.desc: A book brings the Titans' thoughts to life and they must fight their own imaginations. Robin tries to make Cyborg and Beast Boy more active by getting a treadmill.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Power Moves/ Staring at the Future
programme.desc: Robin gets carried away when he and Cyborg start merging their powers and technology; Cyborg and Beast Boy have a staring contest that lasts 30 years.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: No Power/ Sidekick
programme.desc: Feeling the other Titans depend too much on their super powers, Robin challenges them to go 24 hours without using their powers; Robin gets called to the Batcave, but it is only to be a house sitter while Batman is away.

programme.title: DC Super Hero Girls: #LivingTheNightmare
programme.desc: Diana, Kara, and Barbara do more than save citizens from Super-Villains.

programme.title: DC Super Hero Girls: #AllAboutZee
programme.desc: Diana, Kara, and Barbara do more than save citizens from Super-Villains.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball: The Internet/ The Plan
programme.desc: Gumball accidentally uploads a video of himself and then demands The Internet take it down... personally! AND The Watterson kids come up with a fool-proof plan to save their mom, but is it Gumball-proof?

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Thank God for Scoliosis
programme.desc: Charlie and Jake fight for the affections of a waitress, while Alan fights the sexual tension between him and his receptionist.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: I Think You Offended Don
programme.desc: Judith is pregnant with a baby girl and Alan is convinced that he is the father. Meanwhile, Jake is in a contentious mood and Charlie thinks he knows why.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: David Copperfield Slipped Me a Roofie
programme.desc: Convinced that Alan's family doesn't fully appreciate him, Melissa invites him to live with her and her mom.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: I'd Like to Start With the Cat
programme.desc: Charlie finds himself in couples counselling.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: She'll Still Be Dead at Halftime
programme.desc: Charlie has to remove a half-naked woman from his bedroom before Chelsea arrives and catches him with another woman.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: The 'Ocu' or the 'Pado'?
programme.desc: When Charlie tells Chelsea that he loves her, he doesn't get the response he expects.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: My Son's Enormous Head
programme.desc: When Chelsea gets sick, Charlie has to take care of her and must fight the overwhelming urge to flee.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: The Two Finger Rule
programme.desc: Charlie joins Alan, Herb and his next-door neighbour in a night of alcohol-fuelled sexual reminiscences, after Chelsea finds nude pictures of another woman on his phone.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Hello, I Am Allan Cousteau
programme.desc: When Evelyn finally meets Chelsea, Charlie tries to prevent them from becoming friends.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Above Exalted Cyclops
programme.desc: Chelsea sets Alan up on a blind date with her new friend, Rose.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Sir Lancelot's Litter Box
programme.desc: Charlie mourns the loss of his bachelor pad after reluctantly agreeing to let Chelsea move in. Meanwhile, Alan finds that there's not much space left for him in the house.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Good Morning Mrs. Butterworth
programme.desc: Alan and Chelsea become best buds and Charlie decides to take advantage of the situation.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Baseball Was Better With Steroids
programme.desc: Charlie starts to question his relationship with Chelsea when he hears that Mia is back in town. Meanwhile, Judith goes into labour and Charlie, Alan and Herb rush her to the hospital.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: 818-jklpuzo
programme.desc: Charlie must decide between his fiancée, Chelsea, and his old flame, Mia.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Whipped Unto the Third Generation
programme.desc: When Charlie's fiancée, Chelsea, convinces him to let Melissa, Alan's girlfriend, move in, the girls take over the house.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: MMM, Fish. Yum
programme.desc: Alan plays nursemaid to Judith's baby and her tipsy mother. Meanwhile, Jake blackmails Charlie with an incriminating photo.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Laxative Tester, Horse Inseminator
programme.desc: Alan takes a job working for Evelyn and Charlie is forced to mend Chelsea and Jake's rocky relationship.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: For the Sake of the Child
programme.desc: Jake refuses to stay with Charlie and Alan after they embarrass him in public.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Give Me Your Thumb
programme.desc: Alan recommends to Chelsea that she get a breast reduction and Charlie kicks him out because of it.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Untainted by Filth
programme.desc: Charlie and Alan wake up together in bed with a strange woman.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Sleepless in Mar Vista
programme.desc: After taking some new sleeping pills, Christine wakes up to discover she made several embarrassing phone calls while knocked out.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Undercover Brother
programme.desc: After sleeping with Richard's wildly attractive brother, Christine learns an astonishing secret.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Strange Bedfellows
programme.desc: Christine attempts to become politically active by volunteering to collect names for an environmental cause. While soliciting signatures, an activist on the opposite side of the issue confronts her and the two find themselves in a bitter battle.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: The Real Thing
programme.desc: Christine almost gets Mr. Harris fired after unintentionally sending all the parents at Ritchie's school an email revealing that their parent/ teacher relationship may be something more. Meanwhile, Christine gets some surprising news from Mr. Harris.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Faith Off
programme.desc: When Christine learns that Richard has been hired to remodel 'meanie mom Lindsay's bathroom, she can't help but intervene and make sure he doesn't screw up the job and risk their reputation at Ritchie's school.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: My Big Fat Sober Wedding
programme.desc: Christine's attempt to stay sober at a friend's wedding doesn't keep her from blurting out a secret.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Empty Nests
programme.desc: Renee is feeling depressed and lonely with her children grown up and moved out and her husband's workaholic habits.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Dorothy's Prized Pupil
programme.desc: Dorothy feels that it's her fault when her prized pupil wins a writing contest which inadvertently signals the INS that he’s an illegal alien.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Son-in-Law Dearest
programme.desc: Dorothy's daughter arrives after she learns that her husband is cheating on her.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: To Catch a Neighbor
programme.desc: Two police detectives stake out the girls' home to investigate the neighbors whom are believed to be dealers in stolen gems.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: A Piece of Cake
programme.desc: While preparing for a friend's birthday party, the girls recall past birthdays, including Rose's surprise birthday party for Dorothy, a surprise birthday party for Blanche and a memory of Sophia's birthday involving her late-husband Sal.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Old Friends
programme.desc: Dorothy learns that Sophia's new friend is suffering from Alzheimer's disease.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: One for the Money
programme.desc: Dorothy and Sophia flashback to their purchase of the family's first television in 1954.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Bringing Up Baby
programme.desc: Rose inherits her late uncle's estate, provided she takes care of his prized pig 'Baby.'

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Housekeeper
programme.desc: The girls hire a new housekeeper and she puts a curse on their house.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Nothing to Fear But Fear Itself
programme.desc: Dorothy and Blanche accompany Rose to her aunt's funeral where she must give the eulogy.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: A Letter to Gorbachev
programme.desc: Rose writes to Soviet leader Gorbachev and receives an invitation to Moscow.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Strange Bedfellows
programme.desc: Blanche is exposed in the newspaper entering the home of a local married politician.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Brotherly Love
programme.desc: Blanche makes a move at Ted, Stan's brother, the minute she learns he's a neurosurgeon. She is furious when she discovers the only woman Ted is apparently interested in is Dorothy. Meanwhile, Rose is suffering from insomnia.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: A Visit From Little Sven
programme.desc: Rose's cousin Sven, arrives in Miami for a visit just prior to him being married by arrangement. However, when Blanche plays a trick on her latest beau, which involves Sven, the naive groom falls for her.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Audit
programme.desc: Dorothy is stunned to learn that Stan's tax problems are her tax problems since Stan is being audited for a number of years when they were still married. Meanwhile, Blanche and Rose take a Spanish class, each for different reasons.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Three on a Couch
programme.desc: Constant bickering causes the girls to seek help from a therapist. This prompts the girls to reminisce about times they didn't get along. The therapist's conclusion: they should not live with one another.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Charlie's Buddy
programme.desc: A wartime buddy of Rose's late husband arrives in Miami to see Rose. However, he has more in mind than just a friendly visit. Meanwhile, Dorothy and Blanche haggle over the same dress to wear to a social event.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Artist
programme.desc: Dorothy, Blanche and Rose all agree to pose for the Hungarian sculptor Laszlo.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Blanche's Little Girl
programme.desc: Blanche is thrilled by a visit from her daughter, Rebecca, an ex-model who has now gained quite a bit of weight. Also visiting with Rebecca is her oafish beau who constantly puts down her weight.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Dorothy's New Friend
programme.desc: Dorothy befriends a novelist, Barbara Thorndyke, and she quickly infringes on Dorothy's relationship with Blanche and Rose, who think Barbara is nothing but a stuck up snob.

programme.title: The Zoo: The Great Turtle Comeback

programme.title: The Zoo: Grumpy Cats' New Digs
programme.desc: Iray, a red ruffed lemur, is rushed to the health center when keepers discover a wound on his leg. A new exhibit is built for the Pallas's cats, the original grumpy cats. Plus, seven adorable baby turtles join the zoo's colony of an endangered species.

programme.title: The Zoo: Bronx Tales: New Chick on the Block

programme.title: The Zoo: Porcupine Blind Date
programme.desc: The zoo opens an endangered turtle species facility, while Malayan tigers Suhanna and Rimba struggle to breed, because Rimba seems disinterested. Needles the porcupine gets a new mate, and the Guar herd eagerly await the arrival of new calves.

programme.title: Saved By the Barn: Aqua Cow
programme.desc: A cow called Dwight is ready to conquer his pain from a leg injury and heads to Indiana for hydrotherapy. Lola the goat is gifted with a prosthetic and is ready to walk again, while Dan takes in over a dozen exotic farm animals.

programme.title: The Aquarium: Baby Beluga Birth

programme.desc: With the arrival of fall comes the great Maine moose season. Warden MacCabe goes on a six day hunt and takes you along for the ride. Also, wardens investigate a double moose kill, one of the worst wildlife crimes there is.

programme.title: North Woods Law: To Serve and Protect

programme.title: North Woods Law: Occupational Hazards
programme.desc: Deep in the Maine woods, danger lurks behind every tree for the game wardens. Warden Herring comes across a sprawling illegal encampment on private property. Warden Stevens discovers three marijuana grows and must seize dozens of plants.

programme.title: North Woods Law: In a Hot Second

programme.title: North Woods Law: The Rescuers
programme.desc: The wardens race against the clock to evacuate an injured hiker on the Appalachian Trail. A fishing license check leads Warden Dan Christianson to a suspicious stash. Things run afoul as Warden Kris MacCabe discovers great blue herons acting strangely.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Hunting the Hunters

programme.desc: Tanner and Kate's flirtations become more obvious to the crew which leads Simone to lose all respect for the two when she finds out they've already kissed. Pulling up to the marina, Kate sees the first yacht she ever worked on as a stewardess.

programme.desc: Captain Lee calls an all staff meeting to address the previous night's debauchery and gives the crew an ultimatum. Brian refuses to apologize for his behaviour at the night market and tells Courtney he is fed up, causing relationship turmoil.

programme.title: Below Deck: Big Girls, Do Cry
programme.desc: Putting the final charter at risk, Kate sets Kevin up for failure by allowing him to present the primary's requested erotic cake knowing she already went to bed. Captain Lee arranges a Full Moon Party on a private beach for the crew's final night.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Patriarch of Perfection
programme.desc: Meet the Chrisley family, where Todd Chrisley is king. He micromanages his family just like his business and always tries to stay five steps ahead of his five children.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Not So Sweet Sixteen
programme.desc: Savannah is turning sweet 16, and a big birthday bash is in the works. Todd has to come to grips with the fact that his little girl is growing into a young woman. At the party, Todd keeps a watchful eye on girl-crazy Chase.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Jugs and Ammo
programme.desc: Todd’s eldest daughter Lindsie broke his heart when she eloped with her now-husband, Will, without telling her family. While Lindsie recovers from plastic surgery, Todd and Will make an effort to find some common ground at the shooting range.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Two Men and a Baby
programme.desc: The Chrisleys welcome a new addition to the family: Baby Chloe. Chloe is Kyle's daughter, and she is coming to live with Todd and Julie while they teach Kyle 'Parenting 101.' Savannah and Chase get into a fender bender in Savannah's new car.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Todd-Tervention
programme.desc: The Chrisley's are off to their South Carolina lake house in celebration of Todd's mother, Faye's, birthday. To his kids' dismay, Todd confiscates all electronic devices to ensure a weekend of real family bonding.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Big Apple Blowup
programme.desc: Todd takes Savannah and Chase to New York City to teach them about the family fashion business. Savannah proves to be a perfect student, but the only thing Chase learns is how to pick up models.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Date Night
programme.desc: The Chrisley kids show their love for their parents by treating them to a special day of pampering and a romantic dinner, but planning isn't the kids' strong suit. What starts off with good intentions ends up in a comedy of errors.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Hey Chloe, It's Your Birthday!
programme.desc: Todd bets Julie he can throw just as fabulous a birthday party for Chloe as she could. The bet is on, but Todd has to do it all on his own. He soon realizes that planning cupcakes and a petting zoo is not as easy as Julie makes it look.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Rules for Dating My Teenage Daughter
programme.desc: When Julie announces she's going back to work, Todd must transform into Mr. Mom and take over the household duties. Meanwhile, Savannah feels like she's ready to start dating and pleads her case to go out with a 19-year-old American Idol.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Misadventures in Babysitting
programme.desc: Todd and Julie head out of town, and leave Chase and Savannah at home to watch Grayson overnight for the first time. They hope they will follow the rules, but Chase and Savannah have plans of their own.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Atlanta: Reunion Part Three
programme.desc: The ladies clear the air surrounding the Cookie Lady and Cynthia and Eva each speak their mind on the situation. Yovanna makes an appearance to clear her name and makes a shocking claim that leaves the ladies speechless.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Grasslands

programme.desc: When satellites spot anomalies in the unexplored Amazonian jungle, experts wonder if they've discovered relics of a lost civilization. An on-the-ground investigation reveals a dark truth that's more sinister than ever imagined.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Curse of Medusa
programme.desc: Experts investigate a strange glowing lake reported to turn things into stone. A mysterious structure in Africa that resembles Stonehenge is said to be the resting place of a mythical giant. A dark conspiracy could be at work in the Australian outback.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Ghost Fleet of Cannibal Bay
programme.desc: See a fleet of sunken ships that point to ghosts, cannibals, and bloodshed in early America; strange Arctic holes that NASA can't explain; and near Area 51, mysterious alien structures straight out of Star Wars.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Amazon's Secret Nightmare
programme.desc: When satellites spot anomalies in the unexplored Amazonian jungle, experts wonder if they've discovered relics of a lost civilization. An on-the-ground investigation reveals a dark truth that's more sinister than ever imagined.

programme.title: How It's Made: Baseboard Heaters, Pulp Containers, Video Games

programme.desc: Elon Musk and experts from SpaceX and NASA are on a mission to revolutionize space exploration. Special inside access reveals how their cutting-edge spacecraft technology will carry humans to the moon, Mars and beyond.

programme.title: Space Launch LIVE: America Returns to Space
programme.desc: NASA and SpaceX join forces to reach a new milestone in space exploration: the first crewed orbital mission launched from U.S. soil in almost ten years. Coverage from Kennedy Space Center goes behind the scenes to reveal history in the making.

programme.title: How It's Made: Nuno Felt, Drum Crushers, Kimchi and Parquet Floors
programme.desc: Featuring nuno felt; drum crushers; kimchi; traditional parquet floors.

programme.title: How It's Made: Curling Stones, Refrigerators, Aluminum Baseball Bats, Opalescent Glass
programme.desc: Find out how curling stones, refrigerators, aluminum baseball bats are made.

programme.title: The Interrogator: Missing on Christmas Eve
programme.desc: A mother's worst nightmare becomes a reality when her 12-year-old son is abducted on Christmas Eve. Detective Waters soon learns this was not the first time the child went missing.

programme.title: The Interrogator: The Murders at Immaculate Auto Sales
programme.desc: Three members of a tight-knit family are brutally murdered in broad daylight while working at their family owned car dealership. After an intense four-month investigation, Fil Waters boils it down to a scorned woman and an eyewitness who saw the killer.

programme.title: The Interrogator: Bonnie and Clyde
programme.desc: When two Yellow Cab drivers are murdered in under a week, the city of Houston is thrown into a frenzy. Detective Fil Waters soon learns that there are recordings of the same female voice luring the drivers to their deaths.

programme.title: The Body in the River
programme.desc: Police in Dawson County, Georgia race to trace a 911 call from a woman being held at gunpoint down by the river.

programme.title: 20/20 on ID Investigates: My Father BTK
programme.desc: A serial killer who escapes justice for 30 years. The community that never forgets. The law enforcement officers who set the trap to catch him, and his daughter who tells of the father she knew and the heartbreaking realization he was the BTK killer.

programme.title: American Monster: Smoke Gets in Your Eyes
programme.desc: When a devastating fire at the Kohnle family home leaves a family member dead, it appears to be a tragic accident. As the evidence mounts up, it's the testimony of two young kids who lead the police to decipher if it was an accident, suicide or murder.

programme.title: 20/20 on ID Investigates: My Father BTK
programme.desc: A serial killer who escapes justice for 30 years. The community that never forgets. The law enforcement officers who set the trap to catch him, and his daughter who tells of the father she knew and the heartbreaking realization he was the BTK killer.

programme.title: American Monster: Live by the Sword
programme.desc: Outside Los Angeles, sword collector Neal Williams is the house-husband while his wife Manling keeps the family afloat. It's a tough but happy life, as their home videos attest. Until Manling returns home to find her family slaughtered - at sword point.

programme.title: The Body in the River
programme.desc: Police in Dawson County, Georgia race to trace a 911 call from a woman being held at gunpoint down by the river.

programme.title: Grave Secrets: Beneath the Stairs
programme.desc: Curiosity overcomes new homeowners when they spot a barrel in a crawlspace. When they open it, they are horrified to find a body.

programme.title: Grave Secrets: What My Mother Knew
programme.desc: Every week Janine Sutphen went to symphony practice. One night, she didn't come home. Her husband reveals to police there's more to this housewife than anyone knew. But very quickly a secret comes out- leading investigators down a dark, twisted path.

programme.title: Deadly Devotion: Supremacist Sin
programme.desc: When 25-year-old Christy Brown falls hard for Dave Mitchamore, she soon learns his love for her is only matched by his devotion to the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas, a violent white supremacist gang.

programme.title: Deadly Devotion: The War Within
programme.desc: A trusting teenager has no idea that her first love is part of a dangerous militia group with terrifying plans to kill.

programme.title: Deadly Devotion: Deadly Deliverance
programme.desc: Carolyn Clark is a loving mother and devout member of her Pentecostal church. But when a power-hungry Bishop turns her beloved church into a violent and evil place, she becomes determined to protect her family at all costs.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Memphis Blues
programme.desc: In July of 2010, NBA star and family man Lorenzen Wright goes missing outside of Memphis, TN. When his dead body is discovered in the woods, investigators find that this case is far from a slam dunk.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: The Springfield Three
programme.desc: On June 6, 1992, Suzie Streeter and Stacy McCall are out celebrating high school graduation. By morning, the teens are missing, along with Suzie's mother. The case leads police on a nationwide search as they work to solve this baffling mystery.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: What Happened to Baby Lisa?
programme.desc: In 2011, a 10-month-old baby girl goes missing from her home in Kansas City. The family fears she's been kidnapped by a drifter, only to be accused by the police of being responsible for her disappearance.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Fallen Angels
programme.desc: In 2002 a 12-year-old girl from Oregon City, OR goes missing on her way to school and the whole town is clamoring to find her. The police investigation offers little in the way of leads, and then the unthinkable happens - a second girl disappears.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Vanished
programme.desc: So-Cal surfer and tech entrepreneur, Chris Smith, dreams of making a tidy sum of money and then surfing off into the sunset. It seems his dreams have come true, but all is not as it appears.

programme.title: Disappeared in the Darkness: An ID Mystery
programme.desc: When police near Myrtle Beach discover an abandoned car at a boat landing, they surmise its owner, twenty-year-old Heather Elvis, has vanished. They soon discover she's been enmeshed in a love triangle with a married man and led down a path of deceit.

programme.title: Brittany Murphy: An ID Mystery
programme.desc: Drugs, disease and scandals when Hollywood superstar Brittany Murphy dies at age 32, the world is stunned. Many believe foul play must be involved. Brittany's father searches for answers as lurid allegations against other family members mount.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil: Bin Laden: A Terrorist Mastermind
programme.desc: See how Osama bin Laden's journey unfolds from his roots as a privileged young Saudi boy to becoming the formidable leader of al-Qaeda, and his lifetime commitment to violent jihad against the West. This resulted in the horrific 9/11 attacks in 2001.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil: Saddam: The Butcher of Baghdad
programme.desc: The story of Saddam Hussein, the sadistic tyrant who ruled Iraq with an utter contempt for humanity for 23 years, a man whose cruelty knows no limits and who'll stop at nothing to achieve the power of life and death over millions of people.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil: Gaddafi: Mad Dog of the Middle East
programme.desc: The story of the world's longest serving dictator, Muammar Gaddafi, and his 42 year reign of terror at home in Libya and around the world. His actions are so increasingly insane that President Reagan dubs him The Mad Dog of the Middle East.

programme.title: The Renegade Mobster
programme.desc: Crazy Joey Gallo psychopathic killer, beatnik hipster and renegade mobster. Burning with rage and determined to make his way to the top, he wages all-out war against the US and sparks a revolution in the mafia before going down in a hail of bullets.

programme.title: Lucky Luciano: Father of the Mob
programme.desc: The story of crime boss Charlie 'Lucky' Luciano, who used his Mafia connections to help the U.S. government during the Second World War in return for his parole.

programme.title: The Last Don
programme.desc: Joe Massino, the 'Last Don', crushes anyone who goes against the mafia code of omerta. Loyalty to his family, and obedience to their code of silence, is everything. But when the Feds take Massino down, Massino takes everyone down with him.

programme.title: Mafia vs. JFK
programme.desc: Florida and Cuba were ruled by top don Santo Trafficante. The King of Cuba's casinos was on the verge of mob stardom when a revolution destroys his plans. It results in a secret alliance between the mob and the CIA and a failed assassination attempt.

programme.title: Lucky Luciano: Father of the Mob

programme.desc: The story of Saddam Hussein, the sadistic tyrant who ruled Iraq with an utter contempt for humanity for 23 years, a man whose cruelty knows no limits and who'll stop at nothing to achieve the power of life and death over millions of people.

programme.title: Gunslingers: Bat Masterson: Defender of Dodge
programme.desc: This well-dressed gunfighter was a gambler, a hunter, an Indian fighter, and a scout; but above all else, Bat Masterson was a lawman. As Sheriff of Ford County, Kansas, Bat Masterson kept the peace on the volatile Frontier.

programme.title: Gunslingers: Deacon Jim Miller: The Pious Assassin
programme.desc: His neighbours saw a devout family man. The last thing his victims saw was a shotgun aimed at their heads. If there's an outlaw who was born to kill its Deacon Jim Miller, the Bible-thumping psycho-killer.

programme.title: Gunslingers: Seth Bullock: Sheriff of Deadwood
programme.desc: In Deadwood, a mining camp swirling with violence and vice, Seth Bullock is the only stabilizing force standing in the way of utter chaos.

programme.title: Cold War Armageddon: Peace and War
programme.desc: By the mid 1970s, the Cold War has remained a fact of life for decades. But 30 years into the war, and the doctrine of mutually assured destruction doesn't feel like a roadmap to peace. It's an arms race, a propaganda and at times, a deadly game.

programme.title: Cold War Armageddon: Revolution
programme.desc: A look at how Russia sealed themselves behind an Iron Curtain, a move that spawned the birth of revolutions across East Europe and beyond.

programme.title: Cold War Armageddon: Warmonger
programme.desc: In the early 1980's, the next battlefield in the struggle between communism and democracy emerges, and it's in the United State's own backyard.

programme.title: Cold War Armageddon: Paranoia
programme.desc: As the propaganda war and the arms race rage on, the 1950's becomes the decade of paranoia. The stalemate between democracy and communism reaches a fever pitch as the world descends further in chaos.

programme.title: Cold War Armageddon: Rise of the Superpowers
programme.desc: For four decades ago, two global superpowers faced off, threatening to annihilate one another with the push of a button. It is an arms race, a propaganda battle and at times, a deadly game.

programme.title: Forbidden History: Killing Mussolini
programme.desc: A look at the once all-powerful Mussolini, friend of the Fuhrer, who ended his days hanging upside down like a grotesque animal carcass in Milan's Piazzale Loreto.

programme.title: Forbidden History: Hitler's Executioner
programme.desc: We reveal the remarkable story of Johann Reichhart, the man who went from German state executioner before WW2 to head up a merciless Nazi killing machine which murdered thousands. In a bizarre twist of fate, Reichhart ended up working for the Allies.

programme.title: Ultimate Warfare: Courage at Sea
programme.desc: The biggest naval battle in history will be decided when a small group of under-armed, ill-prepared US ships find themselves in a chaotic and ferocious fight against the largest battleships ever built.

programme.title: Cake Wars: Star Wars

programme.title: Bizarre Foods America: Charleston, South Carolina
programme.desc: Andrew steps beyond the carriage rides and stuffy plantation tours to see what really makes the Charleston food scene tick.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods America: Savannah
programme.desc: Andrew makes a trip to the coastal crossroads of the South Savannah, Georgia, where the food in influenced by rich culture.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Santa Monica
programme.desc: Andrew dives in to the laid-back eats of Santa Monica, California. Boasting miles of coastline, Santa Monica is home to spicy seafood soup, Chinese chicken salad, Tsukemen and many other dishes any seafood lover could want.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Mexico City
programme.desc: Mexico City has the most people of any city in the western hemisphere, and they all love to eat. Home to some of the best street food on Earth, Andrew Zimmern explores tasty traditions like barbacoa, chicharrone and huarache.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Denver
programme.desc: Andrew explores the frontier fare of Denver, Colorado. From classic game meats remade for the modern palette to the signature green chilli sauce of Den-Mex cuisine, Denver has become the region's cultural hub with an abundance of top-shelf food.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Zurich
programme.desc: Andrew takes us to Zurich, the origin of some of Switzerland's most famous foods. From warm gooey fondue to Swiss chocolate made with a modern twist, this city is home to some of the most indulgent comfort foods in the world.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Minneapolis, MN
programme.desc: Casey Webb travels to Minnesota's largest city, where he tries a legendary burger that's become a regional icon, noshes on a gigantic brunch meal served with its own food Ferris wheel and takes on a massive four-pound banh mi behemoth.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Saint Paul, MN
programme.desc: Casey's latest delicious detour brings him to Saint Paul for an indoor food truck park, a timeless diner and a terrible twosome of crepes and Thai rolled ice cream.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Worcester, MA
programme.desc: Casey Webb lands in Worcester, MA, where he slurps down some radical ramen, indulges in peanut butter cup French toast and takes on a rack of deep-fried ribs with the heat of a million Scoville units.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation: Baltimore, MD
